:::{#def-ph-partial-lik}
#### Cox PH partial likelihood

$$
\ba
\Lik^*_i(\vb) &\eqdef \frac{\red{\hazfactor(\vx_{(i)})}}{\sum_{k \in R(t_i)} \red{\hazfactor(\vx_k)}}
\\
\Lik^*(\vb) &\eqdef
\prod_{\set{i:\ d_i = 1}} \Lik^*_i(\vb)
\ea
$$

where, for the distinct event times $t_1 < \cdots < t_K$ of the data set:

- $(i)$ is the subject who fails at $t_i$ (unique under the no-ties assumption);
- $R(t_i) \eqdef \set{j : Y_j \ge t_i}$ is the risk set at $t_i$ (where $Y_j = \min(T_j, C_j)$ is subject $j$'s observed follow-up time) — the subjects still under observation just before $t_i$;
- $d_i$ is the number of events at $t_i$ (so $d_i = 1$ when there are no ties);
- $\hazfactor(\vx) \eqdef \expf{\tp{\vx}\vb}$ is the hazard ratio (risk score) for covariate pattern $\vx$.

:::
